description: A sheet or joined strip kept rolled and opened by unrolling is a 'roela' (scroll). It may carry writing or images, or still be blank. The word says how the carrier is stored; its age and content belong to the particular scroll.
articulatory_notes: Tap or briefly trill 'r' against the alveolar ridge and release into middle-height back rounded 'o'. Keep the stressed middle-height front 'e' as its own hiatus syllable. Touch the tongue tip to the ridge for 'l' and complete the word with open central 'a'.
usage_notes: 'pelua' (paper) can name the material, while 'shelu' (book) names a written work gathered as one whole. A scroll may carry a book, record, sign, or drawing without becoming a separate kind of content.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Rolling let a long writing surface be stored and carried before the bound book became common. The format preserved many kinds of text, not wisdom alone.

semantic_domains.communication: a rolled carrier that can hold writing or images
semantic_domains.physical: a sheet or strip stored by winding it around itself or a support
